text: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard. [Entrepreneurial story](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/ondernemershalen/index.html). [Retail](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/retail/index.html). 21/10/2025 • 5 minutes reading. [Henk Hofstede](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/henk-hofstede/index.html). Appel & Ei puts second-hand fashion in a new light with a fresh look and a range that looks like new. “That is our strength,” says director Martin Pot. The store chain, nominated for the ABN AMRO Sustainable Retail Award 2025-2026, strives for growth and scalability to make second-hand fashion the new standard. “Is this second-hand clothing?” is written in sleek white letters on the store window of [Appel & Ei](https://appelenei.nl/) in Gouda.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

It is precisely this sense of wonder that the retail chain wants to evoke.

[Retail](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/retail/index.html). 21/10/2025 • 5 minutes reading. [Henk Hofstede](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/henk-hofstede/index.html). Appel & Ei puts second-hand fashion in a new light with a fresh look and a range that looks like new. “That is our strength,” says director Martin Pot. The store chain, nominated for the ABN AMRO Sustainable Retail Award 2025-2026, strives for growth and scalability to make second-hand fashion the new standard. “Is this second-hand clothing?” is written in sleek white letters on the store window of [Appel & Ei](https://appelenei.nl/) in Gouda. It is precisely this sense of wonder that the retail chain wants to evoke. On the racks you will hardly find second-hand clothing, brought in by consumers, that can hardly be distinguished from new.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: Appel & Ei puts second-hand fashion in a new light with a fresh look and a range that looks like new. “That is our strength,” says director Martin Pot. The store chain, nominated for the ABN AMRO Sustainable Retail Award 2025-2026, strives for growth and scalability to make second-hand fashion the new standard. “Is this second-hand clothing?” is written in sleek white letters on the store window of [Appel & Ei](https://appelenei.nl/) in Gouda. It is precisely this sense of wonder that the retail chain wants to evoke. On the racks you will hardly find second-hand clothing, brought in by consumers, that can hardly be distinguished from new. Only the most beautiful and current pieces pass the strict inspection.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: The store chain, nominated for the ABN AMRO Sustainable Retail Award 2025-2026, strives for growth and scalability to make second-hand fashion the new standard. “Is this second-hand clothing?” is written in sleek white letters on the store window of [Appel & Ei](https://appelenei.nl/) in Gouda. It is precisely this sense of wonder that the retail chain wants to evoke. On the racks you will hardly find second-hand clothing, brought in by consumers, that can hardly be distinguished from new. Only the most beautiful and current pieces pass the strict inspection. The contributor receives 40 percent of the sales price of a sold item.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: It is precisely this sense of wonder that the retail chain wants to evoke. On the racks you will hardly find second-hand clothing, brought in by consumers, that can hardly be distinguished from new. Only the most beautiful and current pieces pass the strict inspection. The contributor receives 40 percent of the sales price of a sold item. Is a piece of clothing still in the store after six weeks?

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: On the racks you will hardly find second-hand clothing, brought in by consumers, that can hardly be distinguished from new. Only the most beautiful and current pieces pass the strict inspection. The contributor receives 40 percent of the sales price of a sold item. Is a piece of clothing still in the store after six weeks? Then it goes back to the owner or to circular textile collector Drop & Loop.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: New fashion or second hand? According to general manager Martin Pot, this puts Appel & Ei in a unique position. "We sell second-hand clothing, but act as new fashion. Our range is of good quality, changes every day and is based on the seasons. We do not sell vintage, but clothing from about two fashion seasons ago.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: Martin Pot. Director Appel & Ei. With this concept, Appel & Ei has steadily grown into a national chain in recent years. metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: With this concept, Appel & Ei has steadily grown into a national chain in recent years. Founder Arda Palm opened the first store in Leiden in 2001, driven by the mission to make people happy with beautiful second-hand clothing. The success of the second store in Doetinchem made it clear that there was growth potential. The only question was: in what form? The choice fell on a franchise formula.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: Founder Arda Palm opened the first store in Leiden in 2001, driven by the mission to make people happy with beautiful second-hand clothing. The success of the second store in Doetinchem made it clear that there was growth potential. The only question was: in what form? The choice fell on a franchise formula. Appel & Ei now has 22 locations, including 18 franchise stores and four own stores.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: The success of the second store in Doetinchem made it clear that there was growth potential. The only question was: in what form? The choice fell on a franchise formula. Appel & Ei now has 22 locations, including 18 franchise stores and four own stores. From 22 to 50 stores.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: The only question was: in what form? The choice fell on a franchise formula. Appel & Ei now has 22 locations, including 18 franchise stores and four own stores. From 22 to 50 stores. After almost 25 years of steady growth, Appel & Ei is now at a crossroads.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: The choice fell on a franchise formula. Appel & Ei now has 22 locations, including 18 franchise stores and four own stores. From 22 to 50 stores. After almost 25 years of steady growth, Appel & Ei is now at a crossroads. The years 2025 and 2026 are important, when the retail chain wants to lay the foundation for further growth and scalability.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: Appel & Ei now has 22 locations, including 18 franchise stores and four own stores. From 22 to 50 stores. After almost 25 years of steady growth, Appel & Ei is now at a crossroads. The years 2025 and 2026 are important, when the retail chain wants to lay the foundation for further growth and scalability. The ambitions are clear: from 22 to 50 stores in 2030.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text:
Martin Pot. Director Appel & Ei
From local to central. The biggest challenge? Uniformity in the sorting and pricing of second-hand clothing. *“*Selecting exactly the right clothing requires a sense of fashion and the market,” Pot explains. "You have to know which fashion is still current and be strict about quality.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text:
Director Appel & Ei
From local to central. The biggest challenge? Uniformity in the sorting and pricing of second-hand clothing. *“*Selecting exactly the right clothing requires a sense of fashion and the market,” Pot explains. "You have to know which fashion is still current and be strict about quality. That is our strength, but monitoring this uniformity is also a challenge.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: The biggest challenge? Uniformity in the sorting and pricing of second-hand clothing. *“*Selecting exactly the right clothing requires a sense of fashion and the market,” Pot explains. "You have to know which fashion is still current and be strict about quality. That is our strength, but monitoring this uniformity is also a challenge. It requires instinctive sense and fashion and price knowledge from our franchisees and store employees.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: Uniformity in the sorting and pricing of second-hand clothing. *“*Selecting exactly the right clothing requires a sense of fashion and the market,” Pot explains. "You have to know which fashion is still current and be strict about quality. That is our strength, but monitoring this uniformity is also a challenge. It requires instinctive sense and fashion and price knowledge from our franchisees and store employees. At the moment, all stores still do the selection themselves.".

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: "You have to know which fashion is still current and be strict about quality. That is our strength, but monitoring this uniformity is also a challenge. It requires instinctive sense and fashion and price knowledge from our franchisees and store employees. At the moment, all stores still do the selection themselves.". That is why Appel & Ei will focus on centralization and economies of scale in the coming years. “We will soon start centralizing everything in one warehouse at a central location,” says Pot. “Consumers bring their clothes to the stores, we pick everything up there, sort and price the items in one central location and then distribute them again to the stores.”.

That sounds simpler than it is.

text: Pot: "We work on consignment with our contributors. If an item is not sold, it often has to be returned to the owner. That makes it complex. Take the cash register system. We now have a basic system, but it is not sufficient for the rollout we have in mind.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: If an item is not sold, it often has to be returned to the owner. That makes it complex. Take the cash register system. We now have a basic system, but it is not sufficient for the rollout we have in mind. Many suppliers of cash register systems drop out as soon as they hear how our model works.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: That makes it complex. Take the cash register system. We now have a basic system, but it is not sufficient for the rollout we have in mind. Many suppliers of cash register systems drop out as soon as they hear how our model works. You have to integrate everything: customer data, the consignment agreements and the settlement of that 40 percent.". > “We will soon start centralizing everything in one warehouse at a central location.”.

text:
You have to integrate everything: customer data, the consignment agreements and the settlement of that 40 percent.". > “We will soon start centralizing everything in one warehouse at a central location.”. Martin Pot. Director Appel & Ei
Sort smarter with AI. According to Pot, technology is also essential to scale up. That is why Appel & Ei is working with partners on an AI tool that can recognize and classify clothing by brand, type and value.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text:
Martin Pot. Director Appel & Ei
Sort smarter with AI. According to Pot, technology is also essential to scale up. That is why Appel & Ei is working with partners on an AI tool that can recognize and classify clothing by brand, type and value. Pot: “That helps us to make selection and pricing more consistent centrally and to guarantee quality.”.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text:
Director Appel & Ei
Sort smarter with AI. According to Pot, technology is also essential to scale up. That is why Appel & Ei is working with partners on an AI tool that can recognize and classify clothing by brand, type and value. Pot: “That helps us to make selection and pricing more consistent centrally and to guarantee quality.”. In addition, the coordination of supply and demand is an important point of attention. “We can be offered more clothing than we can sell,” Pot explains.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: According to Pot, technology is also essential to scale up. That is why Appel & Ei is working with partners on an AI tool that can recognize and classify clothing by brand, type and value. Pot: “That helps us to make selection and pricing more consistent centrally and to guarantee quality.”. In addition, the coordination of supply and demand is an important point of attention. “We can be offered more clothing than we can sell,” Pot explains. "But how do you get the right item, at the right time, in the right store?

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: That is why Appel & Ei is working with partners on an AI tool that can recognize and classify clothing by brand, type and value. Pot: “That helps us to make selection and pricing more consistent centrally and to guarantee quality.”. In addition, the coordination of supply and demand is an important point of attention. “We can be offered more clothing than we can sell,” Pot explains. "But how do you get the right item, at the right time, in the right store? That's what it's all about.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: Pot: “That helps us to make selection and pricing more consistent centrally and to guarantee quality.”. In addition, the coordination of supply and demand is an important point of attention. “We can be offered more clothing than we can sell,” Pot explains. "But how do you get the right item, at the right time, in the right store? That's what it's all about. That's why our stores only accept fall clothing in the fall.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text: In addition, the coordination of supply and demand is an important point of attention. “We can be offered more clothing than we can sell,” Pot explains. "But how do you get the right item, at the right time, in the right store? That's what it's all about. That's why our stores only accept fall clothing in the fall. We try to convey that well to our contributors.".

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text:
"But how do you get the right item, at the right time, in the right store? That's what it's all about. That's why our stores only accept fall clothing in the fall. We try to convey that well to our contributors.". But what if it also includes beautiful summer pieces? “We don't accept them now, because our stores are too small to store them,” Pot answers. “If we start working centrally, I see an opportunity to accept these items and put them back in stores at the right time.”
Franchise or own stores?

That's what it's all about. That's why our stores only accept fall clothing in the fall. We try to convey that well to our contributors.". But what if it also includes beautiful summer pieces? “We don't accept them now, because our stores are too small to store them,” Pot answers. “If we start working centrally, I see an opportunity to accept these items and put them back in stores at the right time.”
Franchise or own stores? How does the role of franchise fit within Appel & Ei's growth ambitions?

That's why our stores only accept fall clothing in the fall. We try to convey that well to our contributors.". But what if it also includes beautiful summer pieces? “We don't accept them now, because our stores are too small to store them,” Pot answers. “If we start working centrally, I see an opportunity to accept these items and put them back in stores at the right time.”
Franchise or own stores? How does the role of franchise fit within Appel & Ei's growth ambitions? Pot: "We have mainly grown through franchisees in recent years, but have now put a stop to new applications.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text:
But what if it also includes beautiful summer pieces? “We don't accept them now, because our stores are too small to store them,” Pot answers. “If we start working centrally, I see an opportunity to accept these items and put them back in stores at the right time.”
Franchise or own stores? How does the role of franchise fit within Appel & Ei's growth ambitions? Pot: "We have mainly grown through franchisees in recent years, but have now put a stop to new applications. The focus is on strengthening the foundation. Only when the central processes are in place will we consider in what form we will continue to grow."
Not 100 percent sustainable.

metadata.source_title: Appel & Ei makes second-hand fashion the new standard

text:
How does the role of franchise fit within Appel & Ei's growth ambitions? Pot: "We have mainly grown through franchisees in recent years, but have now put a stop to new applications. The focus is on strengthening the foundation. Only when the central processes are in place will we consider in what form we will continue to grow."
Not 100 percent sustainable. Although second-hand is obviously sustainable, Pot emphasizes that Appel & Ei does not profile itself as a sustainable retailer. *“*Sustainability makes sense to us, but you also have to be honest: we are not 100 percent sustainable.

Pot: "We have mainly grown through franchisees in recent years, but have now put a stop to new applications. The focus is on strengthening the foundation. Only when the central processes are in place will we consider in what form we will continue to grow."
Not 100 percent sustainable. Although second-hand is obviously sustainable, Pot emphasizes that Appel & Ei does not profile itself as a sustainable retailer. *“*Sustainability makes sense to us, but you also have to be honest: we are not 100 percent sustainable. Because our operation also emits C0₂.
